title: Tailors and dressmakers
description: All installation, maintenance, and repair workers not listed separately. Perform technical activities at power plants or individual installations necessary for the generation of power from geothermal energy sources. Monitor and control operating activities at geothermal power generation facilities and perform maintenance and repairs as necessary. Install, test, and maintain residential and commercial geothermal heat pumps. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Repair or replace defective garment parts, such as pockets, zippers, snaps, buttons, and linings; repair or replace worn or defective parts or components, using hand tools; and inspect articles for defects and remove damaged or worn parts, using hand tools.

Develop, copy, or adapt designs for garments, and design patterns to fit measurements, applying knowledge of garment design, construction, styling, and fabric; confer with customers to determine types of material and garment styles desired; and discuss design specifications with designers.
Estimate how much a garment will cost to make, based on factors such as time and material requirements; and record information about work completed and machine settings.
Position patterns of garment parts on fabric, and cut fabric along outlines, using scissors; and trace outlines of specified patterns onto material, and cut fabric, using scissors.
Sew buttonholes and attach buttons to finish garments; and sew garments, using needles and thread or sewing machines.
Put in padding and shaping materials; and make garment style changes, such as tapering pant legs, narrowing lapels, and adding or removing padding.
Test patterns by making and fitting sample garments; and make adjustments to patterns after fittings.

Draw outlines of pattern parts by adapting or copying existing patterns, or by drafting new patterns; and create a paper pattern from which to mass-produce a design concept; and create a master pattern for each size within a range of garment sizes, using charts, drafting instruments, computers, or grading devices.

Mark samples and finished patterns with information, such as garment size, section, style, identification, and sewing instructions; and mark cutting lines around patterns or templates, or follow layout points, using squares, rules, and straightedges, and chalk, pencils, or scribes.

Compute dimensions of patterns according to sizes, considering stretching of material; and examine sketches, sample articles, and design specifications to determine quantities, shapes, and sizes of pattern parts, and to determine the amount of material or fabric required to make a product.
Trace outlines of paper onto cardboard patterns, and cut patterns into parts to make templates; and cut, shape, and trim materials, such as textiles, using knives, scissors, and other hand tools.
Confer with coworkers to obtain information about orders, processes, or problems; and monitor and adjust operations of machines or systems in garment production.
Fit and study garments on customers to determine required alterations; and inspect products to ensure that quality standards and specifications are met.

Sew, join, reinforce, or finish parts of articles, such as garments, using needles and thread or other materials; and use different sewing techniques such as felling, tacking, basting, embroidery, and fagoting.
Trim excess material, using scissors; and trim excess threads or edges of parts, using scissors or knives.
Create design specifications to provide instructions on garment sewing and assembly; and record required alterations and instructions on tags, and attach them to garments.
Press garments, using hand irons or pressing machines; and maintain garment drape and proportions as alterations are performed.

Align and stitch or glue materials such as fabric, fleece, leather, or wood, to join parts; and construct, decorate, or repair leather products according to specifications, using sewing machines, needles and thread, leather lacing, glue, clamps, hand tools, or rivets.
Drill or punch holes and insert or attach metal rings, handles, and fastening hardware, such as buckles; and extract information about orders, processes or problems from coworkers.
Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed; and notify supervisors of mechanical malfunctions.
Adjust cutting techniques to types of fabrics and styles of garments; and select thread, twine, cord, or yarn to be used, and thread needles.
Fit garments on clients, altering as needed; and examine tags on garments to determine alterations that are needed.
Separate materials or products according to size, weight, type, condition, color, or shade; and read work orders to determine dimensions, cutting locations, and quantities to cut.

Trim excess material or cut threads off finished products, such as cutting loose ends of plastic off a manufactured toy for a smoother finish; and unroll, lay out, attach, or mount materials or items on cutting tables or machines.
Determine the best layout of pattern pieces to minimize waste of material, and mark fabric accordingly; and stack cut items and load them on racks or conveyors or onto trucks.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures; and plan and develop new products and production processes to improve garment making efficiency.

Determine standards, budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies, equipment and labor availability, and workloads. Recommend or implement measures to motivate employees and to improve production methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency. Maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results. Interpret specifications, blueprints, job orders, and company policies and procedures for workers. Enforce safety and sanitation regulations.
